National — Healthcare (current, from national.org.nz/healthcare)

Quality, Timely Healthcare. National is delivering more local doctors, nurses, life-saving medicines, and easier access to healthcare. Priority is getting treatment when you need it. With a record $30 billion annual investment, rebuilding the system around patients, holding it accountable, driving better outcomes, and boosting the frontline.

So far delivered:
- More doctors, faster access: more NZ-trained doctors in primary care so you can see a GP faster; more doctor training places at medical school each year; overseas-trained doctors allowed to work in GP practices; additional funding for GP practices.
- Free breast screening extended to women aged 70–74, supported by a new digital platform that identifies eligible women, sends invitations, and enables secure online booking.
- Better digital access: a new 24/7 digital health service that has delivered over 20,000 phone/laptop GP consultations since July 2025; get prescriptions, lab test referrals and radiology online.
- Investment in hospitals: building new modern hospitals (e.g. Nelson, Dunedin) in record time; upgrading hospitals and clinics nationwide; 38 new/replacement CT, MRI and SPECT scanners over three years.
- Delivering better mental health services.

Approach: more frontline staff, more medicines funded (via Pharmac), faster access and shorter wait times, accountability and health targets, modern infrastructure and digital services.
